question: Can the latest stable upstream PdfPig package publish as a trimmed .NET 10 Native-AOT binary and deterministically extract non-empty text from all three qualified PDF fixtures, including the permission-encrypted fixture, twice each within 5 seconds and 256 MiB peak resident memory?
context: Blocks the production PDF extraction choice in DD-INGEST-DOCUMENT-EXTRACTION. Success requires a published linux-x64 binary, no source path in process arguments, identical per-fixture text digests across two runs, all fixtures readable without an opening password, and each invocation below the INGEST NFR bounds.
decision: Use upstream PdfPig 0.1.14 in-process. A published .NET 10 Native-AOT spike extracted all three qualified fixtures twice with identical digests; worst observed run was 0.37 seconds and 51,416 KiB RSS. The legacy package failed under trimming and is not permitted.
